techniques.evidence_quote: targeting AWS accounts and most of the time caused by a leaked IAM user access key
created a new administrator IAM user with a console profile
the attacker created two additional administrator IAM users, along with a login profile and an access key
the attacker attempted to access several EC2 instances through EC2 Instance Connect … SendSSHPublicKey
the attacker attempted to run EC2 instances in an unused region, us-west-1

techniques.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Firewall
techniques.evidence_quote: ec2:AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress — Open security group to 0.0.0.0/0 on port 22
Most of the IP addresses observed had a history of residential proxy activity, most likely to evade traditional deny-list IP threat intelligence defenses
iam:ListUsers — 'How many people are using this account?'

they used the console to enumerate a number of elements such as EC2 instances running in the account, volumes, security groups, and load balancers. They also leveraged the AWS Resource Explorer to list resources
the attacker manually clicked on several S3 buckets to view objects inside them, browsed their directory tree … generating ListObjects / HeadObject / GetObject events
ses:GetSendQuota … ses:ListIdentities … enumerate a number of elements such as EC2 instances running in the account, volumes, security groups, and load balancers
the attacker attempted to access several EC2 instances through EC2 Instance Connect … SendSSHPublicKey
downloaded files, generating ListObjects / HeadObject / GetObject events … kms:Decrypt — Triggered by AWS automatically because the file was KMS-encrypted

Created multiple ECS Fargate clusters … Ran malicious containers in these clusters, by creating ECS task definitions … Scaled each task definition using an ECS service, to ensure that each task definition runs 25 tasks
The Docker images … contain an entrypoint, /run.sh, that downloads XMRig and starts mining cryptocurrency
